title: alopecurus pratensis subsp. nigricans
intro: Alopecurus pratensis subsp. nigricans, commonly known as black meadow grass or black foxtail, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Alopecurus pratensis subsp. nigricans include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive problems, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional remedies as a poultice or topical application.
